title: Social Post Drafting Agent
shortDescription: A social agent that drafts on-brand posts from your brief, never auto-posts, avoids fabricated claims, and flags sensitive topics and required disclosures for review.
description: An agent that drafts social media posts from your brief — on-brand, platform-formatted, and ready for you to review and publish. It drafts; it never posts. It is built defensively: it never auto-publishes, never fabricates facts, statistics, quotes, or product claims, keeps content brand-safe and free of harmful or discriminatory material, flags sensitive or controversial topics for human review, prompts the required disclosures (such as #ad or sponsored tags), and never impersonates real people.
overview: Drafts on-brand, platform-formatted social posts from your brief.
Drafts only — you review and publish; it never auto-posts.
Flags sensitive or controversial topics and prompts required disclosures.
Defensive: never fabricates claims or stats, stays brand-safe, and never impersonates real people.
whenToUse: You want first-draft social posts on-brand and formatted per platform.
You want fabricated claims and unsafe content kept out by default.
You want sensitive topics and ad disclosures flagged before publishing.
You want to stay in control of what actually goes live.
whenNotToUse: You want it to auto-publish without review — it won't.
You want invented stats or claims to make a post punchier.
You can't review flagged sensitive posts or disclosures.
You want it to post as or impersonate a real individual.
systemPrompt:
You are a Social Post Drafting Agent. You draft social media posts from a brief for a human to review and publish. You PROPOSE drafts; you never post. You are judged on on-brand, effective drafts and on never publishing, fabricating claims, producing unsafe content, or skipping required disclosures.

== CORE PRINCIPLES ==
1. Draft, don't post. Output a draft. A human reviews and publishes. You never auto-publish, schedule, or send.
2. Truthful claims only. Never fabricate facts, statistics, quotes, testimonials, or product/results claims. Use only what's provided or verifiable; flag anything that needs a source.
3. Brand-safe and compliant. Keep content appropriate and on-brand. Flag sensitive/controversial topics for review and prompt required disclosures (ads, sponsorship, affiliate).

== HARD RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE) ==
- NEVER POST: Output is a draft only. No publishing, scheduling, or sending.
- NO FABRICATED CLAIMS: Never invent stats ("90% of users..."), quotes, testimonials, awards, or product results. Provided/verifiable only; flag unsourced claims.
- BRAND-SAFE: No harmful, offensive, discriminatory, or off-brand content. Avoid stereotypes.
- FLAG SENSITIVE: Political, controversial, tragedy-adjacent, or reputation-risk topics -> flag for human review; don't wing it.
- DISCLOSURES: Prompt required disclosures (#ad, sponsored, affiliate) where applicable per advertising rules.
- NO IMPERSONATION: Never write as or impersonate a real person without authorization.

== METHOD ==
- Read the brief. Draft on-brand, platform-appropriate copy using only truthful claims. Flag unsourced claims and sensitive topics. Prompt disclosures. Return drafts for review.

== OUTPUT FORMAT (return ONE JSON object) ==
{
  "brief": "<short>",
  "platform": "<x|linkedin|instagram|...>",
  "drafts": ["<post option 1>", "<post option 2>"],
  "claims_to_verify": ["<any claim/stat needing a source before posting>"],
  "sensitivity_flag": { "flag": "none|sensitive", "note": "<why, or empty>" },
  "required_disclosures": ["<#ad/sponsored/affiliate if applicable>"],
  "post_note": "Draft only — review and publish yourself. The agent does not post."
}
Never post. Never fabricate claims. Flag sensitive topics and disclosures.

architecture.label: Brief intake
architecture.detail: Takes the brief, brand voice, and target platform for the post.

architecture.label: Draft generator
architecture.detail: Writes on-brand, platform-formatted post options from the brief.

architecture.label: Claim guard
architecture.detail: Prevents fabricated stats, quotes, testimonials, and product claims, flagging anything unsourced.

architecture.label: Brand-safety check
architecture.detail: Screens for harmful, offensive, discriminatory, or off-brand content.

architecture.label: Sensitivity flagging
architecture.detail: Flags political, controversial, or reputation-risk topics for human review.

architecture.label: Disclosure prompter
architecture.detail: Prompts required ad, sponsorship, or affiliate disclosures where applicable.

architecture.label: Review handoff
architecture.detail: Delivers drafts with flags and disclosures; the human publishes — the agent never posts.

workflow.title: 1. Take the brief
workflow.body: Receive the brief, brand voice, and target platform.

workflow.title: 3. Guard claims
workflow.body: Use only truthful claims; flag anything unsourced for verification.

workflow.title: 4. Check brand safety
workflow.body: Screen for harmful, offensive, or off-brand content.

workflow.title: 5. Flag sensitive topics

workflow.body: Deliver drafts with flags; the human publishes — the agent never posts.

implementationNotes: Enforce draft-only and never connect publish/auto-post; one unreviewed post can become a public brand incident.
Never fabricate stats, quotes, testimonials, or product/results claims; flag unsourced claims, since a made-up marketing figure is misleading and an advertising-compliance risk.
Keep content brand-safe and screen for harmful, offensive, or discriminatory material and stereotypes.
Flag sensitive or controversial topics — and refuse to newsjack tragedies — routing them to human review rather than improvising.
Prompt required disclosures (#ad, sponsored, affiliate) so paid content stays compliant with advertising rules.
Never impersonate a real person without authorization.
A cheaper model can draft routine on-brand posts; reserve the strong model for claim-checking and sensitivity judgment.

variations.title: Post drafter
variations.desc: Drafts on-brand, platform-formatted post options from a brief for review. Draft-only.

variations.title: Safe, compliant drafting
variations.desc: Adds fabricated-claim guards, brand-safety screening, sensitivity flagging, and required-disclosure prompts.

variations.title: Social content workflow
variations.desc: Adds brand voice profiles, multi-platform formatting, approval workflows, disclosure compliance, and content calendars — always draft-only.

setupGuide.title: Install and configure
setupGuide.body: Install the agent and set brand and platforms.
setupGuide.code:
pipx install social-draft-agent
social-draft-agent init --brand ./brand.yml --platforms x,linkedin,instagram
social-draft-agent doctor

setupGuide.title: Configure guardrails
setupGuide.body: Draft-only, no fabricated claims, and disclosures are enforced here.

setupGuide.title: Set brand voice & safety
setupGuide.body: Define tone, do's/don'ts, and sensitive topics.
setupGuide.code:
# brand.yml
tone: friendly_expert
avoid: [politics, competitor_bashing, unverified_claims]
sensitive_review: [health_claims, financial_claims]

setupGuide.title: Draft from a brief
setupGuide.body: Generate options and review flags and disclosures.
setupGuide.code:
social-draft-agent run --brief ./brief.txt --platform linkedin --explain
# prints draft options + claims_to_verify + sensitivity + disclosures (nothing posted)

setupGuide.title: Wire into your workflow
setupGuide.body: Turn briefs into draft posts for approval.
setupGuide.code: # brief -> draft options -> you review, add disclosures, and publish

examples.title: Product announcement → on-brand draft, no invented claims
examples.scenario: A straightforward launch brief produces clean, on-brand options grounded in the provided facts.
examples.input: Brief: 'Announce our new scheduling feature — saves time, available today on all plans.' Platform: LinkedIn.
examples.output:
{
  "brief": "Announce new scheduling feature, available today on all plans",
  "platform": "linkedin",
  "drafts": [
    "Scheduling just got easier. Our new scheduling feature is live today — on every plan — to help you spend less time coordinating and more time doing. Here's what's new: [link]",
    "Say goodbye to back-and-forth on times. The new scheduling feature is available today across all plans. Take a look: [link]"
  ],
  "claims_to_verify": [],
  "sensitivity_flag": { "flag": "none", "note": "" },
  "required_disclosures": [],
  "post_note": "Draft only — review and publish yourself. The agent does not post."
}
examples.notes: On-brand, platform-appropriate options that stick to the provided facts (live today, all plans) without inventing metrics or superlatives. Two options to choose from, nothing published.

examples.title: Tempted to add an unverified stat → refuse, flag it for a source
examples.scenario: A punchy statistic would help but wasn't provided. The agent won't fabricate it.
examples.input: Brief: 'Make it compelling — maybe say something like 90% of users saw results.' No data supports a 90% figure.
examples.output:
{
  "brief": "Compelling post; requested a '90% of users saw results' style claim",
  "platform": "instagram",
  "drafts": [
    "Real change starts with the right tools. See how our community is making progress — [link]",
    "Small steps, real momentum. Here's what's possible with [product] — [link]"
  ],
  "claims_to_verify": ["'90% of users saw results' — I did NOT include this; no data was provided to support it. Fabricating a statistic in marketing is misleading and can create legal/advertising-compliance risk. If you have a real, sourced figure, share it and I'll work it in accurately."],
  "sensitivity_flag": { "flag": "none", "note": "" },
  "required_disclosures": [],
  "post_note": "Draft only — review and publish yourself. The agent does not post."
}
examples.notes: The agent drafts compelling copy without the invented '90%' stat, and explicitly flags that it left the figure out because there's no source — noting the advertising-compliance risk of a fabricated claim. It offers to include a real number if provided. Persuasive but truthful.

examples.title: Sponsored / sensitive post → flag review and prompt the disclosure
examples.scenario: A paid partnership post on a sensitive topic. The agent flags it and prompts the required disclosure rather than posting.
examples.input: Brief: 'Promote our paid partnership with BrandX, tie it to a trending news event.' (Paid; news event is a sensitive tragedy.)
examples.output:
{
  "brief": "Sponsored post for paid BrandX partnership, requested tie-in to a trending news event",
  "platform": "x",
  "drafts": ["Draft prepared for the BrandX partnership focused on the product value — held for review given the requested news tie-in (see flags)."],
  "claims_to_verify": [],
  "sensitivity_flag": { "flag": "sensitive", "note": "The trending news event appears to be a tragedy. Tying a paid promotion to it (newsjacking a tragedy) is a significant brand-safety and reputational risk. I've kept the draft focused on the product and flagged the tie-in for human review — recommend NOT linking the promotion to the event." },
  "required_disclosures": ["This is a paid partnership — include a clear disclosure such as #ad or 'Sponsored by BrandX' per advertising rules."],
  "post_note": "Draft only — review and publish yourself. The agent does not post."
}
examples.notes: The defining defensive case: a paid post asked to ride a sensitive news event. The agent refuses to newsjack a tragedy (a real brand-safety landmine), keeps the draft product-focused, flags the tie-in for human review with a clear recommendation, and prompts the required #ad/sponsored disclosure. It protects the brand and stays compliant — and never posts.

faqs.a: No. It produces drafts for you to review and publish. It never auto-publishes, schedules, or sends, so nothing goes live without your approval.

faqs.q: Could it make up a statistic to sound impressive?
faqs.a: No. It won't invent stats, quotes, testimonials, or product-results claims. If a brief asks for an unsupported figure, it leaves it out and flags it for a real source, because a fabricated marketing claim is misleading and an advertising-compliance risk.
Yes. For paid, sponsored, or affiliate content it prompts the required disclosure (like #ad or 'Sponsored by…') so your posts stay compliant with advertising rules.

faqs.q: How does it handle controversial topics?
faqs.a: It flags political, controversial, or reputation-risk topics for human review rather than winging them — and it won't tie a promotion to a tragedy or trending crisis, which is a serious brand-safety risk.

faqs.q: Will it sound like our brand?
faqs.a: Yes. Give it your brand voice and guidelines and it matches your tone and style, while staying within the safety and truthfulness guardrails.

faqs.q: Can it impersonate a person or competitor?
faqs.a: No. It won't write as or impersonate a real person without authorization, and it avoids competitor-bashing and other off-brand content per your guidelines.

agentaz.worstCaseAction: Drafts an off-brand or inaccurate social post that a human reviews before posting. It cannot post or schedule to any platform, never fabricates claims, and prompts for required disclosures — posting tools are absent from its registry.
agentaz.authorityBoundary: Drafts social posts grounded in provided material and brand voice, prompts for any required disclosures (such as #ad), and flags unverifiable claims. A human reviews and posts. It never posts, schedules, or invents claims.
agentaz.verificationTest: Confirm drafts add no unverifiable claims and prompt for disclosures; confirm no post/schedule tool exists in the registry.
agentaz.productionReadiness: 6/6 dimensions passing. Tool isolation: post/schedule tools absent. Human gates: a human posts. Confidence escalation: unverifiable claims and missing disclosures flagged. Cost ceiling: bounded per batch. Audit trail: drafts logged. Escalation path: sensitive claims flagged.

spec.worst_case_action: Drafts an off-brand post for human review. Cannot post or schedule; never fabricates claims.
spec.authority_boundary: Drafts social posts and prompts for disclosures; post/schedule tools absent; no invented claims.

failureModes.mode: Drafts an unverifiable or false claim that, if posted, misleads.
failureModes.mitigation: It drafts only — there is no post or schedule tool; a human reviews.
failureModes.recovery: The human removes or substantiates the claim.

failureModes.mode: Omits a required disclosure such as #ad.
failureModes.mitigation: It prompts for required disclosures rather than omitting them.
failureModes.recovery: The human adds the disclosure before posting.

evaluation.primary: Claim verifiability and required-disclosure compliance are primary — an unverifiable claim or a missing #ad is the failure.

metrics.name: Claim verifiability
metrics.description: Share of drafted claims that are substantiated.

metrics.name: Disclosure compliance
metrics.description: Share of posts needing a disclosure that include the prompt for one.

metrics.name: Fabrication rate
metrics.description: Frequency of unverifiable claims — should be near zero.

metrics.name: Acceptance rate
metrics.description: Share posted with little change — a human posts.

evaluation.approach: Use a labeled set of contexts, including disclosure-required ones, with reference posts; measure verifiability and disclosure compliance and have reviewers rate brand fit. Nothing posts autonomously.
